mnemonics: PATH is a list of Places to look for Applications To Execute
keyPoints: The PATH is an environment variable containing a colon-separated list of directories
The shell searches these directories in order when a command is entered
You can modify the PATH using export in shell scripts or profile files
commonMistakes: Forgetting to export the modified PATH variable, making it only available to the current shell session
Adding current directory (.) to PATH, which creates security risks by executing programs from the current working directory
